load_assumptions.mvp: Small teams, moderate supplier files, quote generation on demand, and no real-time collaboration requirement.
load_assumptions.future: Higher import volume, scheduled supplier sync, customer portals, AI enrichment, analytics, and public sharing traffic.

scaling_strategy.strategy: Scale stateless API runtime horizontally when traffic grows.
Scale import, document, notification, sync, and AI workers independently by queue depth.
Use indexed workspace-scoped queries, read models for search-heavy screens, and archival for old import rows.
Use signed URLs, lifecycle policies, and separate generated output storage from source uploads.
Keep deterministic calculation inputs explicit and snapshot approved outputs.
Use feature flags, quotas, async processing, source-grounded prompts, and human review before approval.

resilience_strategy: Retry transient worker failures with capped backoff.
Make import and document jobs idempotent where possible.
Persist job status and error details for user-visible recovery.
Prevent third-party outages from corrupting source-of-truth data.
Use operational console for failed imports and connector diagnostics.

scalability_risks.risk: Asset search can become slow as product and service libraries grow.
scalability_risks.mitigation: Start with relational search/indexes and preserve a later search-service extraction path.

scalability_risks.risk: Import history can grow quickly and bloat operational tables.
scalability_risks.mitigation: Separate import staging tables and apply retention/archival policy.

scalability_risks.risk: Future AI enrichment can create cost and latency spikes.
scalability_risks.mitigation: Keep AI async, quota-limited, feature-flagged, and non-authoritative.

architecture_decisions.decision: Do not introduce dedicated search, analytics, or AI infrastructure in MVP.
architecture_decisions.rationale: Stage 2 validation supports deferring these until usage justifies operational complexity.

architecture_decisions.decision: Use queue depth and job failure rates as early scaling signals.
architecture_decisions.rationale: Import, document, and future connector workloads are the most likely early bottlenecks.

tradeoffs.choice: Relational search first, dedicated search later
tradeoffs.cost: May need migration when asset volume and search complexity grow.
Requires UX states for progress and completion.

stage_4_handoff_notes: Design loading, progress, retry, and empty states for import, document generation, and search.
stage_5_handoff_notes: Add queue/job tests for retry, idempotency, and failure visibility.
Instrument job duration and failure metrics from MVP.
